seo.title: Building Auth provider comparison and selection with Cler...
seo.description: A step-by-step implementation guide for Auth provider comparison and selection with Clerk and Auth.js.

content.intro: This guide implements a secure authentication and authorization system with multi-tenant support, OAuth integration, and API protection. Focuses on practical implementation patterns for full-stack developers.
prerequisites: Selected auth provider (Auth.js, Clerk, or Supabase Auth)
Basic understanding of JWT and OAuth 2.0

steps.title: Initialize auth provider integration
steps.explanation: Set up your chosen auth provider SDK with environment variables. Configure domain, API keys, and callback URLs. Verify provider-specific setup requirements (e.g., Clerk dashboard, Auth.js middleware).

code.content:
export AUTH_PROVIDER=authjs
export NEXTAUTH_URL=http://localhost:3000
export AUTH_SECRET=your-32-byte-secret

pitfalls: Exposing API keys in client-side code
Using development URLs in production environments

steps.title: Implement multi-tenant schema
steps.explanation: Add tenant identifier to database models. Create middleware to extract tenant context from subdomain or request headers. Configure database connection pooling per-tenant if needed.
pitfalls: Forgetting to index tenant identifiers in database queries
Sharing session storage across tenants without isolation

steps.title: Configure OAuth provider integration
steps.explanation: Register application with identity providers (Google, GitHub, etc.). Implement OAuth callback handlers with state validation. Store access tokens securely in database with scope restrictions.
pitfalls: Allowing implicit grant flows without PKCE
Not validating state parameters in OAuth callbacks

steps.title: Implement token-based session management
steps.explanation: Use opaque session tokens with refresh tokens stored in secure HTTP-only cookies. Implement token revocation patterns for logout and password changes.
pitfalls: Using JWTs for session storage without proper signing configurations
Failing to invalidate tokens on password reset

steps.title: Secure API endpoints
steps.explanation: Add middleware to verify JWT tokens and enforce role-based access control. Implement rate limiting per-user and per-tenant. Validate all request inputs against schema.
pitfalls: Missing CORS configuration for API endpoints
Not validating content-type headers for JSON payloads

content.conclusion: This implementation provides a secure foundation for authentication and authorization with multi-tenant support. Verify all security configurations through penetration testing and maintain regular audits of access control policies.
